requirements.label: Supervise local sessions remotely
requirements.statement: Remote pi operators shall be able to supervise local pi agent sessions from Telegram while execution remains on the originating computer.
requirements.rationale: The intended purpose identifies continuity of control as the product's core value: the operator should be able to babysit a local pi session from a phone without moving execution off the local machine.

validation.criteria: Review the intended purpose and user-facing workflow to confirm a paired operator can observe and control a local pi session from Telegram while the session's tools, files, credentials, and runtime remain on the local computer.

requirements.label: Control sessions mid-turn
requirements.statement: Remote pi operators shall be able to connect to and control a pi session even when an agent turn is already running.
requirements.rationale: The operator may leave while a turn is active; connecting only before a run starts would fail the babysitting use case at the moment it matters most.

validation.criteria: Review a representative active-turn workflow and confirm Telegram connection during the turn gives the operator useful activity visibility and a way to influence or stop the active work.

requirements.label: Direct multiple sessions
requirements.statement: Remote pi operators shall be able to distinguish and direct work across multiple concurrently connected pi sessions.
requirements.rationale: The intended use includes several pi sessions running across projects; the operator needs session awareness and routing rather than a single ambiguous bot conversation.

validation.criteria: Review the Telegram-facing session workflow to confirm the operator can identify available sessions, tell which one is selected or addressed, and direct work to the intended session without ambiguity.

requirements.label: Choose steer or follow-up
requirements.statement: Remote pi operators shall be able to choose whether a message sent during a busy turn steers current work or becomes follow-up work.
requirements.rationale: Remote control is lifecycle-sensitive: urgent corrections should steer the active turn, while non-urgent next steps should wait until the current run completes.

validation.criteria: Review the documented Telegram command behavior to confirm ordinary messages sent while a selected session is busy default to queued follow-up work, and that explicit steer controls let the operator redirect a still-queued follow-up into the active turn without ambiguity.

requirements.label: See progress and results
requirements.statement: Remote pi operators shall receive enough live activity and final-response feedback in Telegram to judge session progress and decide whether to intervene.
requirements.rationale: Babysitting requires more than final notifications; the operator needs progress visibility that preserves the meaning of thinking, tool use, stopping, failure, and completion.

validation.criteria: Review a representative long-running session transcript or demo to confirm the Telegram view shows current activity, preserves important history, and delivers a non-duplicated final answer.

requirements.label: Exchange files safely
requirements.statement: Remote pi operators shall be able to exchange relevant Telegram files and generated artifacts with local pi sessions while protecting unrelated local files, obvious secrets, and downloaded Telegram data from unintended exposure.
requirements.rationale: Remote supervision often requires showing the agent an image, document, or voice-derived instruction and receiving generated files, but Telegram files and local artifacts cross a trust boundary that must remain privacy-aware.

validation.criteria: Review inbound-file and outbound-artifact workflows to confirm relevant Telegram files can reach the local session, generated artifacts are sent back only when pi explicitly attaches them, downloaded Telegram data is treated as private, and obvious secret or unrelated local paths are not exposed unintentionally.

requirements.label: Preserve recoverable delivery
requirements.statement: Remote pi operators shall not lose queued work, final responses, or session routing continuity because of ordinary Telegram retry windows, update redelivery, transient automatic reconnect windows, or broker turnover.
requirements.rationale: The bridge exists for remote continuity during recoverable transport and broker failures, but the project owner clarified that closed or dead pi sessions should clean up their temporary Telegram views because durable session history lives locally.

validation.criteria: Review failure-mode evidence for Telegram rate limiting, duplicate updates, broker takeover, and bounded automatic reconnect to confirm pending turns, final responses, and still-live session routes remain intelligible and recoverable without preserving Telegram topics for closed or dead sessions beyond the reconnect grace window.

requirements.label: Pair one controlling user
requirements.statement: Remote pi operators shall be able to pair exactly one Telegram user through an explicit setup flow, and only that paired user shall control connected pi sessions.
requirements.rationale: The bridge connects a personal Telegram account to powerful local agent sessions; stakeholder trust depends on a clear single-operator pairing model rather than open or team-wide bot access.

validation.criteria: Review setup and message-handling behavior to confirm one Telegram user is paired through the documented setup flow and that only this paired user can route commands, files, steering, follow-ups, or stop requests into connected sessions.

requirements.label: Keep authority local-first
requirements.statement: Remote pi operators shall keep code execution, shell access, workspace authority, credentials, and model/session configuration on the local computer while using Telegram as the control surface.
requirements.rationale: The product is a local-first remote control bridge, not a hosted agent service or remote IDE; preserving this boundary protects scope, trust, and operational simplicity.

validation.criteria: Review architecture and setup documentation to confirm Telegram is used for control and message/file exchange while execution authority and workspace state remain local.

requirements.label: Evaluate constrained changes
requirements.statement: Repository maintainers shall be able to evaluate Telegram bridge changes against the Bot API constraints and remote-supervision purpose they affect.
requirements.rationale: The bridge depends on Telegram-specific constraints that are easy to regress; maintainers and coding agents need durable guidance that ties API details back to the product purpose.

validation.criteria: Review maintainer guidance and planning artifacts to confirm a future change can be checked against the intended purpose and the relevant official Telegram Bot API constraints without relying only on chat history.

requirements.label: Connect and clean sessions
requirements.statement: Remote pi operators shall be able to connect and disconnect pi sessions from Telegram reachability without restarting the project, the bot, or unrelated connected sessions, and disconnected or dead sessions shall stop presenting a Telegram view after any automatic reconnect grace expires.
requirements.rationale: Remote babysitting must fit sessions that start, stop, reconnect, and resume across projects; Telegram reachability is connection-scoped while local pi session history remains on the machine.

validation.criteria: Review the documented connection lifecycle to confirm a session can become reachable from Telegram, can be disconnected or cleaned up after death without restarting the pi project or bot, and does not disrupt unrelated connected sessions or bounded automatic reconnect recovery.

requirements.label: Avoid external broker daemon
requirements.statement: Remote pi operators shall be able to use Telegram supervision without maintaining a separate relay server, external broker service, or extra daemon outside the pi extension.
requirements.rationale: The project owner explicitly wants the extension itself to own brokering so the user does not have to install, operate, or trust extra external software beyond pi and Telegram.

validation.criteria: Review setup and operating documentation to confirm the normal bridge workflow uses the pi extension itself for brokering and does not require a separate relay server, external broker service, extra daemon, or inbound workstation endpoint.

requirements.label: Reload from Telegram in place
requirements.statement: Remote pi operators shall be able to reload a supervised pi session from Telegram without losing the Telegram conversation route used to control that session.
requirements.rationale: Deprecated 2026-04-25: Telegram-triggered runtime reload is intentionally removed because the current pi extension API does not expose a safe direct reload API outside command handlers, and the previous command-injection workaround could surface internal commands in Telegram.

validation.criteria: Deprecated: no Telegram reload workflow is currently expected; review should confirm /reload is not advertised or handled by the Telegram bridge.

refs: Telegram Bot API command messages and sendMessage route/thread behavior

requirements.label: Clean connection-scoped views
requirements.statement: Remote pi operators shall be able to treat Telegram topics and routes as temporary connection-scoped views that are cleaned up when a pi session explicitly disconnects, terminally closes without a successful replacement handoff, dies, or fails to reconnect automatically.
requirements.rationale: The project owner clarified that Telegram is a temporary interface into local pi work, not the durable session history store. A terminal disconnect or close should remove the Telegram view, while user-initiated pi session replacement can continue the same remote supervision relationship when the replacement starts successfully.

validation.criteria: Review the session lifecycle design and behavior to confirm Telegram topics/routes disappear after explicit disconnect, terminal process close, crash/death, or expired automatic reconnect grace, while successful user-initiated replacement can preserve Telegram reachability for the replacement session without making topics durable session history.

requirements.label: Keep passive updates non-alerting
requirements.statement: Remote pi operators shall be able to keep Telegram supervision views updated with passive activity updates and local-input echoes without receiving notification alerts for those passive messages.
requirements.rationale: The operator may be working locally while Telegram is connected or may only need passive progress visibility. Alerting for local input echoes and routine activity updates creates notification noise without adding useful control value, while final answers and explicit command/error replies may still warrant normal notification behavior. Assistant text streaming is no longer planned as a normal supervision surface.

validation.criteria: Review a connected-session workflow to confirm passive activity updates and local-input echoes remain visible in Telegram without notification alerts, and that final answers plus explicit operator-facing command or error replies remain able to notify normally.
